steps.title: Identify the logical relationship
steps.content: Sentence 1: Tang's ambitions "weren't very lofty" — he built the site just because everyone had one. Sentence 2: the site "has become an essential resource" for professionals. The relationship is contrast: a low-ambition origin led, unexpectedly, to a high-impact outcome. The transition must signal that contrast.

steps.title: Eliminate each choice
steps.content:
A: Nevertheless, — signals contrast despite what came before. "He had no lofty ambitions; nevertheless, his site became essential." Matches the contrast perfectly. Correct.

B: Therefore, — signals a logical result or consequence. Low ambition does not logically cause the site to become essential — the opposite is surprising, not expected. Eliminated.

C: Furthermore, — signals addition of a similar point. There is no addition here; the second sentence contradicts the first. Eliminated.

D: Next, — signals a sequence in time or steps. The passage is not narrating a series of steps; it is making a contrasting observation. Eliminated.
